name: Renderer Duration Field Support

tasks.title: Add DurationMS field to Record struct
tasks.description: Add DurationMS field to Record struct
tasks.body: In internal/logrender/record.go, add a DurationMS *int64 field to the Record struct with json tag duration_ms. Use a pointer so zero-value (absent field) is distinguishable from an explicit 0. Place the field near the other timing-related fields (after ExitCode is a natural spot).
acceptance_criteria: Record struct has a DurationMS pointer-to-int64 field with json duration_ms tag
ParseRecord correctly populates DurationMS from JSON input containing duration_ms

tasks.title: Update renderers to prefer pre-computed duration
tasks.description: Update renderers to prefer pre-computed duration
tasks.body: In internal/logrender/summary.go and internal/logrender/interleaved.go, modify the stage_complete and planning_complete handlers: if the record DurationMS is non-nil, convert it to time.Duration and use that instead of computing r.Timestamp.Sub(startTime). If DurationMS is nil, fall back to the existing timestamp-diff logic. This ensures backward compatibility with older log files that lack the field. Apply the same pattern in both Replay and Follow code paths.
acceptance_criteria: Renderers use DurationMS when present in the record
Renderers fall back to timestamp diff when DurationMS is nil
Both Replay and Follow code paths are updated

tasks.title: Add renderer tests for duration_ms consumption
tasks.description: Add renderer tests for duration_ms consumption
tasks.body: Write or extend tests for the summary and interleaved renderers to verify: (1) when a stage_complete record contains duration_ms, the rendered duration matches the pre-computed value (not the timestamp diff), (2) when a stage_complete record lacks duration_ms, the rendered duration is computed from timestamps as before, (3) ParseRecord correctly round-trips a duration_ms field. Use the existing test patterns in internal/logrender/.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies pre-computed duration_ms is used when present
Test verifies timestamp fallback when duration_ms is absent

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Renderer Duration Field Support is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify backward compatibility: renderers must still produce correct output from old log files without duration_ms

breadcrumbs.text: Added DurationMS *int64 field to Record struct with json:duration_ms tag; added tests for present, zero, and absent cases
Wired resolveDuration into all six stage_complete/planning_complete handlers across summary.go and interleaved.go; renderers now prefer DurationMS when present, fall back to timestamp diff when nil
task-0003: Verified all DurationMS renderer tests already exist from prior tasks; 11 tests across 4 files cover preference, fallback, and round-trip scenarios; all pass

Audit reviewed 8 files (~620 lines of production and test code). PASS. One finding remediated: gofmt alignment violation in record.go introduced when DurationMS field was added to the Record struct (task-0001); fixed during audit. Two findings accepted: (1) no ADR for the DurationMS-over-timestamp preference decision, accepted because the logic is a 7-line unexported helper with clear doc comments and the choice is self-evident; (2) no spec for resolveDuration, accepted for the same reason. No correctness, concurrency, or security issues. Test coverage is thorough: 11 DurationMS-specific tests across 4 files cover preference, fallback, zero-value, and absent scenarios. The resolveDuration helper centralizes duration logic cleanly across 6 call sites in summary.go and interleaved.go. Race detector passed. Build clean. Vet clean.

audit.result_summary: Added DurationMS *int64 field to the logrender Record struct for structured timing data, created a resolveDuration helper that prefers pre-computed DurationMS over timestamp subtraction, and wired it into all six completion handlers across summary.go (Replay, Follow) and interleaved.go (Render) for both stage_complete and planning_complete record types. Comprehensive test coverage spans 11 DurationMS-specific tests across duration_test.go, record_test.go, summary_test.go, and interleaved_test.go, verifying preference, fallback, zero-value, and absent-field behavior. Audit found and fixed one gofmt alignment violation; no correctness or concurrency issues.

audit.objective: Verify all work in the renderer-duration-field-support node is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Reviewed 8 files (~620 lines). Found one gofmt violation in record.go where struct field alignment broke when DurationMS was inserted between pointer and value types. Fixed it during audit. Verified all 6 resolveDuration call sites, all 11 DurationMS tests, build, vet, race detector. Two minor findings (missing ADR and spec for resolveDuration) accepted as the code is self-documenting.
went_well: Prior tasks produced clean, well-factored code with thorough test coverage
The resolveDuration abstraction is exactly right: simple, tested, used consistently
improvements: Task-0001 should have run gofmt after modifying the struct; the alignment drift was a mechanical oversight
action_items: None; all work is complete and correct

task-0001.objective: Add a DurationMS *int64 field to the Record struct for duration_ms JSON deserialization
task-0001.what_happened: Added DurationMS *int64 with json:"duration_ms" tag to Record in internal/logrender/record.go, placed after ExitCode. Added three test cases in record_test.go covering present, zero-value, and absent duration_ms.
went_well: Pointer semantics naturally distinguish absent from zero, and ParseRecord's two-pass unmarshal handles the new field with no code changes needed
improvements: None; this was a minimal struct addition
action_items: Downstream tasks should wire DurationMS into renderers that display timing information

task-0002.objective: Update renderers to prefer pre-computed DurationMS over timestamp subtraction
task-0002.what_happened: Added resolveDuration helper in duration.go that checks DurationMS first, falls back to Timestamp.Sub(startTime). Rewired all six completion handlers: stage_complete and planning_complete in summary Replay, summary Follow (handleFollowRecord), and interleaved Render (handleRecord). Also cleaned up the local variable pattern, extracting start-time lookup into t/ok variables before passing to resolveDuration.
went_well: The helper centralizes the duration logic cleanly; six call sites collapsed to one-liners
Table-driven tests for resolveDuration cover all edge cases: DurationMS present, zero, nil with/without start time
improvements: None significant; the change was straightforward
action_items: If new record types with duration semantics are added, they should also call resolveDuration rather than computing timestamps inline

task-0003.objective: Add renderer tests verifying duration_ms consumption behavior
task-0003.what_happened: Found that all required tests were already written by task-0001 and task-0002. record_test.go has TestParseRecord_DurationMS covering present/zero/absent round-trip. summary_test.go has four DurationMS tests (Replay, Follow, PlanningDurationMS, NilFallsBack). interleaved_test.go has three DurationMS tests (Stage, Planning, NilFallsBack). duration_test.go has TestResolveDuration with five cases. All 11 DurationMS-related tests pass.
went_well: Prior tasks produced thorough test coverage as part of implementation, leaving nothing for this dedicated test task to add
action_items: None; all acceptance criteria are satisfied by existing tests
